Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a temporal profile model for an insurance claim handling process The model shows average times and standard deviations between pairs of activities We are to identify anomalies generate hypotheses for why they exist and propose SQL queries to verify these anomalies

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Steps 1 Identify anomalies in the temporal profile model 2 Generate hypotheses for each anomaly 3 Propose SQL queries to verify the anomalies

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Anomaly 1 R P Average time 1 04 days 90000 seconds with a very low standard deviation of 1 hour 3600 seconds This is suspicious because the standard deviation is very low compared to the average meaning the time between Receive and Approve is very consistent which might indicate an automated or rigid schedule that does not account for the complexity of the process

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This might indicate an automated or rigid schedule that does not account for the complexity of the process

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Anomaly 2 P N Average time 7 days 604800 seconds with a high standard deviation of 2 days 172800 seconds This is a long delay with high variability which might indicate a bottleneck or inconsistent process in notifying customers after approval

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This might indicate a bottleneck or inconsistent process in notifying customers after approval

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Anomaly 3 A C Average time 2 hours 7200 seconds with a standard deviation of 1 hour 3600 seconds This is a relatively quick closure after assignment which might indicate that some claims are closed without going through the necessary steps like Evaluate and Approve or that these steps are being skipped

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This might indicate that some claims are closed without going through the necessary steps like Evaluate and Approve or that these steps are being skipped

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Anomaly 4 E N Average time 5 minutes 300 seconds with a standard deviation of 1 minute 60 seconds This is a very quick transition from Evaluate to Notify which might indicate an automated notification system that triggers immediately after evaluation but it might be too fast if there are other steps required like approval in between

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This might indicate an automated notification system that triggers immediately after evaluation but it might be too fast if there are other steps required like approval in between

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: However note that the temporal profile model does not necessarily capture direct transitions It captures the time between the first occurrence of activity1 and the first occurrence of activity2 in the process regardless of what happens in between

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are also given a database schema to work with We will use the claim events table to verify the actual times between activities

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For each anomaly we can compute the actual time between the two activities for each claim Compare with the expected range average plus minus k times standard deviation to find outliers

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We will use k equals 2 for a rough cutoff about 95 percent confidence if normal distribution

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We will also break down by adjuster claim type etc to see if there are patterns

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The claim events table has a timestamp column for each event We need to get the first occurrence of each activity for a claim

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We can use MIN with CASE statements to get the first timestamp for each activity

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Example for R P SELECT claim id MIN CASE WHEN activity equals R THEN timestamp END AS R timestamp MIN CASE WHEN activity equals P THEN timestamp END AS P timestamp FROM claim events GROUP BY claim id HAVING MIN CASE WHEN activity equals R THEN timestamp END IS NOT NULL AND MIN CASE WHEN activity equals P THEN timestamp END IS NOT NULL

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then we can compute the difference and filter by the expected range

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: However note that the temporal profile model might be built on the entire history but we are looking for deviations We can compute the actual average and standard deviation for the entire dataset and then compare with the model or we can look for individual claims that are outside the expected range

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Since the prompt asks to identify specific claims where the time between activities falls outside expected ranges we will do that

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We will use a common table expression to get the timestamps for each activity per claim then compute the difference and filter

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We will also join with the claims table to get additional information like claim type customer id claim amount and with the adjusters table if needed via the resource in claim events

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The resource column in claim events might indicate the adjuster or system that performed the activity We can join with adjusters on adjuster id if the resource is an adjuster but note that the resource might be a system or a different entity We will assume that for the A activity the resource is the adjuster id

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The prompt says to correlate with adjusters claim types etc We will do that in the queries
